title: rubus macrophyllus subsp. eglandulosus
intro: Rubus macrophyllus subsp. eglandulosus is a subspecies of the large-leaved bramble plant, scientifically known as a type of blackberry, specifically native to China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anticoagulant and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular and inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Rubus macrophyllus subsp. eglandulosus include reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and anti-aging properties. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to support cardiovascular health and prevent various age-related diseases.
